news.summary:
Pope Leo on Saturday urged global leaders to avoid dividing their electorates with “sterile simplifications” to gain popularity and called on them to listen to the world’s cries for peace, in a forceful speech opening a week-long tour of Spain.

news.headline: As Ebola Spreads in East Africa, Will China Step Up?
news.summary: China is well positioned to help stop the deadly virus, and could move into a gap left by U.S. retreat.

news.headline: Bernadette Chirac, former first lady of France, dies aged 93
news.summary: Bernadette Chirac, the steel-willed former first lady of France who spent 12 years at the Élysée Palace from 1995 to 2007 beside President Jacques Chirac while building her own political power in rural Corrèze and turning a children’s hospital charity into a national institution, has died. news.headline: Ukraine launches fresh drone attack on St. Petersburg region on final day of ‘Russian Davos’
news.summary: Ukraine on Saturday fired hundreds of drones targeting the St. Petersburg region in the second such attack on Russia’s second-largest city in less than a week. news.headline: A Viral Gen-Z Protest Movement Draws Thousands to India’s Capital
news.summary: The Cockroach Janta Party started as a joke, but it quickly became a way for many Indians to express their frustration with the system.

news.headline: French Open women's final set to make history as Chwalinska faces Andreeva
news.summary: The French Open women's final is guaranteed to make history, with Poland's Maja Chwalinska aiming to become the first qualifier to win the title and Russia's Mirra Andreeva seeking to become the youngest champion since 1992. The match takes place on Saturday at Roland Garros. FRANCE 24's Jamaes Vasi

news.headline: Radio station in DR Congo seeks to stop misinformation amid spread of Ebola
news.summary: The rare Bundibugyo type of Ebola that Congo is battling took locals by surprise after weeks of spreading unnoticed.
